# Tenu.World

> Tenu is a tenant-facing tool that scores rental deposit risk from photos and generates ready-to-send dispute letters. Live on the web at tenu.world for tenants in France; public launch 15 July 2026. United Kingdom support is built but disabled at launch. Operated by Global Apex.Net (SAS, France).

Tenu.World turns a batch of move-in or move-out photos into two deliverables: a **risk analysis report** estimating how much of the deposit a landlord could reasonably withhold based on observed wear, and an optional **template dispute letter** pre-formatted for the jurisdiction (lettre recommandée avec accusé de réception in France). The service is document generation, not legal advice. Legal output in French and English only; user interface in 10 languages including Arabic and Urdu.

## Core

- [Homepage](https://tenu.world/): product overview, three-step flow, pricing summary
- [Pricing](https://tenu.world/pricing): entry scan from EUR 15 (studio) to EUR 35 (house), dispute letter EUR 20 — all prices TTC, VAT included
- [Stories](https://tenu.world/stories): real deposit-dispute case studies
- [Features](https://tenu.world/features/evidence): photo evidence builder, AI risk scan, dispute letters, rights explainers
- [Legal documents](https://tenu.world/legal): privacy, terms, refund policy — French and English

## Pricing (France, TTC)

- Entry scan at move-in: Studio/T1 EUR 15 · T2 EUR 20 · T3 EUR 25 · T4 EUR 30 · T5+/maison EUR 35
- Exit-only scan (no entry record): EUR 25 flat
- Dispute letter add-on: EUR 20
- Colocation: per tenant at the flat's tier; a couple sharing one flat counts as one tenant
- 14-day withdrawal right waived at checkout via explicit consent (art. L221-28 1° Code de la consommation)

## Legal

- [Privacy Policy (FR)](https://tenu.world/legal/privacy/fr) · [(EN)](https://tenu.world/legal/privacy/en): processing, retention, sub-processors, RGPD rights
- [Terms of Service (FR)](https://tenu.world/legal/terms/fr) · [(EN)](https://tenu.world/legal/terms/en): template-not-advice clause, mediation path, French law
- [Refund Policy (FR)](https://tenu.world/legal/refund/fr) · [(EN)](https://tenu.world/legal/refund/en): L221-28 digital-goods waiver, refund scenarios

## Key facts

- Operator: Global Apex.Net SAS, SIREN 941 666 067, RCS Versailles, registered office 4 Boulevard du Chateau, 78280 Guyancourt, France
- Status: live on the web (friends-and-family cohort); public launch 15 July 2026; iOS and Android apps to follow
- Jurisdiction at launch: France only. UK (TDS/DPS/MyDeposits) built, enabled post-launch
- AI provider: Anthropic Claude (Haiku for photo analysis, Sonnet for letter drafting); user photos are never used to train models
- Data residency: EU (Cloudflare R2 EU, Supabase eu-central-1 Frankfurt)
- Contact: support@tenu.world (general), dpo@tenu.world (data protection)

## Optional

- [GitHub](https://github.com/malikmubashir/tenu-world): repository
